description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular, stacked-stone wall forming the main body, set atop a multi-tiered stone platform. The well is topped with a pitched, blue-tiled wooden roof supported by sturdy wooden beams, reinforced with metal brackets. A wooden crank handle and a rope mechanism are attached to one side, allowing a wooden bucket to be lowered into the well. On the opposite side, a large wooden wheel is visible, likely part of the pulley system for drawing water. The stonework is rough and irregular, giving the well a rustic, hand-built appearance. The overall structure is robust and designed to withstand outdoor urban environments.
description: A rustic stone water well with a blue-tiled wooden roof, crank handle, and bucket, set on a stone platform. Features a visible pulley wheel and sturdy wooden supports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, blue-tiled roof, wooden crank handle, and bucket hanging over the opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the bucket, rope, and the angled roof, with the crank handle visible on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the stone well, blue-tiled roof, and the large wooden pulley wheel on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the large wooden wheel and the stone structure, with the bucket less visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: No cover; the well opening is exposed beneath the roof structure.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Present; a wooden crank handle, rope, and bucket are attached to a pulley wheel on the side.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well opening; roof supported by wooden beams.
